product_overview: Orka AI is a multi-agent personalized learning backend that provides streaming chat tutoring, sandboxed code execution, background web research (Korteks), and adaptive quiz management via REST/SSE APIs. The platform orchestrates agent workflows, caches transient context in Redis, and persists canonical data in SQL.
core_goals: Provide a low-latency, streaming chat experience that routes user messages through the Agent Orchestrator to the tutor swarm.
Offer secure authentication with access and refresh tokens for protected API access and token rotation.
Expose a sandboxed code execution endpoint whose results are recorded to Redis and can be used to enrich tutoring/evaluation flows.
Run background research jobs (Korteks) that synthesize web content into markdown reports and make job status/results available to clients.
Support adaptive quiz retrieval and handling while reducing race conditions between multiple quiz-generation paths and ensuring research context can be injected into quiz generation.
Return clear, consistent success and error responses so clients can implement robust happy-path and error handling.

features.name: Authentication API
features.description: Register users, obtain access/refresh tokens, refresh access tokens, and revoke refresh tokens to secure other API endpoints.
user_flows: POST /api/auth/register with valid {email, password} -> Receive 201 Created with userId and basic profile
POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials -> Receive 200 OK with {accessToken, refreshToken, expiresIn}
POST /api/chat/stream with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and body {sessionId, message} -> Receive 200 OK and SSE stream (protected endpoint accessible with token)
POST /api/auth/refresh with valid refreshToken -> Receive 200 OK with new {accessToken, refreshToken}
POST /api/auth/logout with valid refreshToken -> Receive 200 OK confirming token revoked
POST /api/auth/register with existing email -> Receive 409 Conflict with error message 'email already in use'
POST /api/auth/login with invalid credentials -> Receive 401 Unauthorized with 'invalid credentials' error
POST /api/auth/refresh with expired or revoked refreshToken -> Receive 401 Unauthorized or 400 Bad Request indicating invalid refresh token
POST /api/auth/logout without refreshToken -> Receive 401 Unauthorized

features.name: Chat API
features.description: Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming endpoint that sends user messages into the Agent Orchestrator and returns a streamed assistant/tutor response with intermediate signals (e.g. [THINKING:], [PLAN_READY], [TOPIC_COMPLETE], [DONE]).
user_flows: POST /api/chat/stream with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and body {sessionId, message} -> Receive 200 OK establishing SSE stream with initial event (e.g. [THINKING:])
POST /api/chat/stream with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and body {sessionId, message} -> Receive SSE chunk events from the server until final [DONE] event indicating response complete

POST /api/chat/stream with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and body {sessionId, message:'/plan'} -> Receive 200 OK SSE stream with [PLAN_READY] events that contain plan fragments and final [DONE] with plan summary
POST /api/chat/stream without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
POST /api/chat/stream with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and malformed body (missing sessionId or message) -> Receive 400 Bad Request with validation error details

POST /api/chat/stream with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and body {sessionId, message} where Orchestrator/agent errors occur -> Receive 200 OK SSE stream that emits an [ERROR]: <message> event and then closes (client should handle and surface error)

features.name: Code Execution API
features.description: Execute user-submitted code in a sandbox (Judge0 CE / Piston), return execution result (stdout/stderr/success), and persist the last piston result to Redis for enrichment of later tutoring/evaluation activities.
user_flows: POST /api/code/run with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and body {sessionId, code, language} -> Receive 200 OK with {stdout, stderr, success:true, executionTimeMs} on successful execution

POST /api/code/run with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and body {sessionId, code with runtime/compile errors, language} -> Receive 200 OK with {stdout:'', stderr:'<error details>', success:false}
POST /api/code/run without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
POST /api/code/run with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and body {sessionId, code that exceeds time limit} -> Receive 504 Gateway Timeout or 422 Unprocessable Entity with execution timeout error

POST /api/code/run with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and valid code -> Receive 200 OK and server writes last piston result to Redis key orka:piston:{sessionId}:last for later retrieval by TutorAgent

features.name: Research API (Korteks)
features.description: Start background research jobs that scrape and synthesize web content into a Markdown report; clients poll job status and fetch the report once the background KorteksAgent completes synthesis.
user_flows: POST /api/korteks/start with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and body {topic, query, depth} -> Receive 202 Accepted with {jobId} indicating the research job started in background
GET /api/korteks/status/{jobId} with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> -> Receive 200 OK with {jobId, phase:'running', progress, result:null} while job is running
GET /api/korteks/status/{jobId} with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> -> Receive 200 OK with {jobId, phase:'completed', result:{report:"<markdown>", sources:[...]}} when job finishes
POST /api/korteks/start without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
POST /api/korteks/start with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and invalid parameters -> Receive 400 Bad Request with validation details
GET /api/korteks/status/{jobId} with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> for unknown jobId -> Receive 404 Not Found
GET /api/korteks/status/{jobId} with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> -> Receive 200 OK with phase:'failed' and error details when background agent fails to synthesize the report

features.name: Quiz API
features.description: Retrieve pending or baseline quiz questions for the session or user. Quizzes are generated by agents (TutorAgent and/or QuizAgent); note: current known limitation — Korteks research may not be automatically injected into quiz-generation context unless the Orchestrator explicitly enriches generation parameters.
user_flows: GET /api/quiz with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and query {sessionId} -> Receive 200 OK with JSON array of quiz questions and metadata (questionId, prompt, choices, timeLimit) — happy path returns questions

GET /api/quiz with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> and query {sessionId} when no pending quiz exists -> Receive 204 No Content (or 404 with explanatory message) indicating there is no quiz to retrieve
GET /api/quiz without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
GET /api/quiz with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> -> Receive 500 Internal Server Error when quiz generation agent encounters an unhandled exception

GET /api/quiz with Authorization: Bearer <accessToken> after Korteks research completes (if Orchestrator enrichment implemented) -> Receive 200 OK with quiz questions that include researchContext in metadata

features.name: Authentication API
features.description: User registration, login, and token management
